mechanism_type: concerted or stepwise cycloaddition between heterodienes/heterodienophiles and pi partners
common_conditions: combine a heterodiene or heterodienophile with a diene or alkene under thermal or Lewis-acid conditions to generate oxygen- or nitrogen-containing cyclohexene/cyclohexane adducts
description_short: Pericyclic or stepwise hetero-[4+2] annulation used to assemble densely functionalized oxygen- and nitrogen-heterocycles.

mechanism_type: formation of quaternary ammonium salts followed by base- or heat-induced beta elimination
common_conditions: exhaustively methylate an amine, convert the ammonium salt to the hydroxide, then induce elimination or degradation to form the least substituted alkene
description_short: Classical degradation of amines through quaternary ammonium salts to reveal or install alkenes with Hofmann selectivity.

transformation_type: remote C-H amination via N-centered radical 1,5-hydrogen abstraction
mechanism_type: radical halogen-atom transfer and cyclization from N-haloamines or related N-radical precursors
common_conditions: generate an N-centered radical from an N-haloamine or Suarez-type precursor to abstract a remote hydrogen, then cyclize to a saturated nitrogen heterocycle
description_short: Remote radical amination family that converts linear amines or related precursors into cyclic amines through 1,5-hydrogen abstraction.

transformation_type: amide contraction to amines via isocyanates
mechanism_type: N-haloamide formation followed by migration to isocyanate and hydrolysis or trapping
common_conditions: convert a primary carboxamide into an amine or trapped carbamate/urea derivative via N-haloamide generation and rearrangement to an isocyanate
description_short: One-carbon contraction of primary amides to amines or isocyanate-derived products under hypohalite or hypervalent-iodine conditions.

transformation_type: phosphonate-based olefination of aldehydes and ketones
mechanism_type: phosphonate carbanion addition followed by oxaphosphetane collapse to alkenes
common_conditions: deprotonate a phosphonate or phosphine oxide derivative and condense it with an aldehyde or ketone to furnish an alkene, often with high E selectivity
description_short: Widely used phosphonate olefination that usually furnishes E-alkenes under milder and more chemoselective conditions than classical Wittig reactions.
